name: Bill Monroe
abstract: William Smith Monroe ( mən-ROH; September 13, 1911 – September 9, 1996) was an American mandolinist, singer, and songwriter who created the bluegrass music genre. For this reason, he is often called the "Father of Bluegrass".

concerts.label: New York Folk Festival: Grassroots to Bluegrass to Nashville Part 2
New York Folk Festival: The Real Thing
Boys of the Lough/ Bill Monroe and The Blue Grass Boys/ Garrison Keillor

about_quotes.speaker: Bela Fleck
about_quotes.evidence: we loved the fact that there are tapes of Charlie Parker and there are tapes of cool bluegrass bands, Bill Monroe, and Flatt & Scruggs

about_quotes.speaker: Buckingham
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Monroe. Hank Williams, and others in the country. I mean those are American art forms, in my opinion.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: But I think it’s useful to keep that distinction except when Bill Monroe wanted to play a blues or something and then he’d get the bass up there and Bill could sing the blues right up there with Bob Wills and Brownie McGee.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023
